texto: Love is like the senses of the body.Imagine you are blind, you can see an object using touch. You can feel around it and see it mentally.Now imagine you can't feel, but can see. You can't feel what you see.You may see love, but not feel love. When you feel love, you also so see it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Blake McCormick: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Love is like the senses of the body.Imagine you are blind, you can see an object using touch. You…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que saude-mental, love, love-hurts pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de love-hurts e lovers.
